description: Species-level unit mappings used by quantitative calibration targets. These mappings document units from the primary literature; they do not by themselves make simulator units physically predictive.

species.pheromone_unit: inferred pheromone map unit proportional to prior ant occupancy
notes: Perna et al. estimate pheromone concentration from tracked ant passages rather than chemical mass.
Tracking intervals are reported in 0.4 s windows; pheromone map correlations use a map 16 s before the tracking event.
The target curve fits individual turning response, not full colony food retrieval or physical pheromone half-life.

species.pheromone_unit: not measured in the traffic holdout
notes: The observed trail section length is L = 17 body lengths.
The paper reports 1 body length is approximately 18 mm for Leptogenys processionalis.
The target curve uses the Figure 4 density-regime Gaussian velocity fits and validates no-jam speed retention, not absolute simulator speed units.

species.pheromone_unit: not measured directly in the pushing probability target
notes: Bridge widths tested in the paper are 10.0, 6.0, 3.0 and 1.5 mm.
The pushing target uses the Figure 3d regression slope J = 0.571 with CI95 half-width 0.057.
This target validates a crowded-traffic body-contact redirect mechanism, not absolute ant speed or pheromone concentration.
